title: We need to convert libpod.conf files in user homedir for cgroupv2
body:
If a user upgrades to a machine that defaults to a cgroups V2 machine
and has a libpod.conf file in their homedir that defaults to OCI Runtime runc,
then we want to change it one time to crun.

runc as of this point does not work on cgroupV2 systems.  This patch will
eventually be removed but is needed until runc has support.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Fixes: https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1752040

@debarshiray PTAL
@mheon @giuseppe @vrothberg @TomSweeneyRedHat @baude @QiWang19 PTAL

do you think we should move the logic to `system migrate` or somehow rewrite the containers configuration to switch the runtime?  Existing containers won't work with cgroups v2 unless they are recreated

Agree with `system migrate`.

We might want to consider sneaking this in after RC1.
Can we trigger a system migrate without breaking every users containers?
One question, lots of test unhappiness.

> Existing containers won't work with cgroups v2 unless they are recreated

Should I be worried about this? :)

Is a reboot followed by a `podman system migrate` enough to get past this? Or do the containers actually have to be recreated?
Fingers crossed, `podman system migrate` should handle it for you.  If @mheon says differently, go with his thinking.
I don't think that we expect existing containers to be broken after the config file change?

@rhatdan `system migrate` is explicitly for things like this - though config file changes have previously been out of its scope, I think it's the right place.

We should discuss rootless config files, though. This is not the only time we're going to hit this problem, I think - we don't have a good way of dealing with them within the package manager, and Podman has no idea if a user intentional set `runc` instead of `crun`...
Changes LGTM, but it looks like you're still fighting the good fight with the CI system
@giuseppe WDYT?
I'm starting to lean toward the suggestion that we not change the config file, but just ignore `runc` with CGroups V2 (but print a warning) for now - until it becomes safe.

> I'm starting to lean toward the suggestion that we not change the config file, but just ignore `runc` with CGroups V2 (but print a warning) for now - until it becomes safe.

I'd not ignore it, I think a warning should be enough.  I don't like much the idea of ignoring an user configuration when explicitly set

Then we blow up everyone in the universe and I would bet the exactl 0 people have actually said they want runc.  This is just because we don't merge the system defaults with user customization.  We need to change the processing of libpod to be grab the system default and then merge user changes.

> Then we blow up everyone in the universe and I would bet the exactl 0 people have actually said they want runc. This is just because we don't merge the system defaults with user customization. We need to change the processing of libpod to be grab the system default and then merge user changes.

good point.  Ok so let's get this in.  LGTM
Green test buttons on this one....
I'd second Tom's request for a bit more debugging, otherwise LGTM.
@mheon @TomSweeneyRedHat Added log message on failure to find crun.
LGTM and happy green test buttons
@mheon @vrothberg @giuseppe @baude PLEASE give this the /lgtm
/lgtm
We should cut an RC2 on Monday with this landed, I think. 1.6.0 final on Friday, maybe?
SGTM.  We'll have @edsantiago back as well.
